search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG106 breathlessness history examination ECG natriuretic peptide echocardiography
candidate_sources_rejected: heart-failure evidence treated as proof of heart failure
automatic imaging, natriuretic peptide, diuretic, oxygen, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Dyspnea has cardiac and non-cardiac causes and the workflow does not establish heart failure.
No investigation, treatment, medicine, oxygen, or referral decision was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with dyspnea undergoing clinician cardiac review; NICE evidence is direct when heart failure is suspected or established.
setting_applicability: Primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment; acute severe dyspnea requires direct urgent assessment.
UAE_applicability: International NICE evidence requires UAE emergency, cardiology, respiratory, laboratory, imaging, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG106 last updated 2025-09-03 was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG106 remains current NICE chronic-heart-failure guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE heart-failure assessment section supports clinician-documented breathlessness history, cardiovascular and respiratory examination, vital signs, and already selected or reviewed ECG, natriuretic peptide, imaging, and laboratory context without diagnosing heart failure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The monitoring section supports documenting function, fluid status, rhythm, deterioration, and arranged review without generating treatment or escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Onset and trajectory, exertional and positional symptoms, chest pain, cough, wheeze, fever, edema, risk factors, vital signs, saturation, examination, ECG, biomarkers, imaging, laboratory results, cause, severity, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
